subject:
Re: warp_tools 2D Particle Series and Relion5: reconstruct vs
 initial model
body:
*Hi Alister,*

thanks. Too bad - I hoped that there was a solution to replace the dummy 
series 😅

What you suggest is exactly what I am doing now: extract 2D + 3D, use 3D 
for reference.


*Re pose and Relion InitialModel:*

As you can see in this example, the IniModel from Relion is no longer 
aligned on the Z axis (whereas the TM reference was).
[image: Untitled 4.jpg]
Of course this is not an issue if you run this with D7 symmetrization 
afterwards (for the T20S). 

However, the IniModel from real C1 particles will be off, too!

Meaning that the TM particle list angles will be "wrong" - at least you 
will need to do global alignment.

Alternatively, I could get the rotation from the TM reference to the 
IniModel and apply it to the particle list...

But that's a lot of work which could be avoided if a simple reconstruction 
was available 😇

I guess the workaround with 3D particles is the best option for now!


Thanks again! :-) 

Philipp.

On Thursday, August 8, 2024 at 11:28:39 PM UTC+2 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi Philipp,
>
> Great question! 
>
> We’re actually already not totally compliant with R5 so even if the tilt 
> series were correct tomo_reconstruct_particle there wouldn’t work.
>
> Could you elaborate on the pose changing from TM? You just mean that if 
> you run initial model in C1 it can come out different?
>
> The machinery is there for reconstructing from 2D… Dimitry and I have 
> discussed adding a reconstruction routine but there is nothing there yet. 
> The best advice right now would probably be to do a 3D particle export then 
> a relion_reconstruct
>
> Hope that helps!
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Aug 8, 2024, at 14:33, 3P1L <philipp...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿*Dear All,*
>
>
>
> This question not only concerns warp_tools but more generally 2D particle 
> series and WARP + relion 5, but I hope somebody has a suggestion for me 😇
>
> In our particular case, we are running relion 5.0-beta-0-commit-84ba16 and 
> warp_tools on linux.
>
> We use a python version of TOMOMAN to export TS to warp_tools and 
> pytom_GPU for template matching.
>
> We then extract the particle series in warp_tools.
>
> When I generate an initial model from the 2D particle series, everything 
> looks fine and I can run a refinement afterwards. [a bit annoying that the 
> particle pose can change relative to the TM reference, but OK ... 😉]
>
> However, having to run an initial model just to check if particle picks / 
> particles are good is a bit cumbersome ...
>
> From the traditional (3d subtomo) workflow, I would just use 
> relion_reconstruct to get the initial reference.
>
> However, when trying to use relion_tomo_reconstruct_particle on my 
> optimization set (the initial one from warp_tools), I only get empty 
> volumes.
>
> I suppose this is bc warp_tools will only link a "dummy_tiltseries.mrc"?
>
> Is there a way to actually link the correct data in a relion-v5 approved 
> way?
>
>
> Are there any other options in v5 + warp_tools to reconstruct the particle 
> series directly (i.e. without alignment)? [as relion reconstruct does not 
> work on 2D particle stacks]
>
> Thanks!
>
> --

> .
>
>
